content.full_text:
SACRAMENTO
HOUSING AND REDEVELOPMENT
AGENCY

October 3, 1991


John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for Informal Assistance, Clarifying Issues Raised in Your August 5, 1991
Letter to Council Member Fargo (Your File No. I-91-089)

Dear Mr. Wallace,

Sacramento Council Member Heather Fargo asked that I review your letter to her, dated
August 5, 1991, and obtain further clarification of some of the issues raised. As Ms. Fargo
indicated to you, her husband is acquiring, for his business, real property located in a
"Survey Area" which is being considered for a new redevelopment project (the North
Sacramento Redevelopment Area). Also, the City is now considering a redistricting plan
which would include a portion of the proposed redevelopment area in Ms. Fargo's district.
If Ms. Fargo cannot vote on adoption or implementation of the North Sacramento
redevelopment plan, the proposed redevelopment area probably should not be included in
her district.

Therefore, we are requesting informal assistance (a) to determine if it is proper for Ms.
Fargo to vote on various aspects regarding the adoption of the proposed redevelopment
plan, including the boundary selection, project area committee approval, designation of land
uses and approval of the preliminary plan, and (b) to determine to what extent, if any, Ms.
Fargo would be constrained from acting on implementation of projects and activities under
the plan if it were adopted. Unfortunately, action must be taken on the redistricting in two
weeks, and we need your assistance prior to that time.

The proposed boundaries of the North Sacramento project area include approximately 1200
acres out of approximately 5000 acres in the Survey Area. The proposed project area is
predominately commercial and industrial in nature. Industrial uses account for 36% of

MAILING ADDRESS: P.O. Box 1834, Sacramento, CA 95812-1834
OFFICE LOCATION: 650 I Street, Sacramento, CA 95814 (916) 444-9210

SACRAMENTO HOUSING AND REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY
John W. Wallace, FPPC
October 3, 1991
Page 2

proposed project area and commercial and office uses account for 28%. The proposed
project area generally follows the Del Paso Boulevard commercial corridor. (A map of the
proposed project area is enclosed.) There are approximately 1700 different property owners
within the proposed project area, some of whom own several parcels. Parcels range in size
from standard residential lots to seven acre parcels. The proposed boundaries were selected
because they encompass the most blighted areas in the Survey Area. The proposed
redevelopment is intended to revitalize the commercial and industrial uses and reverse
blighting trends throughout the area.

Ms. Fargo's husband is acquiring an industrial parcel of approximately one-half acre, located
at the corner of Colfax and El Monte (2132 Colfax as shown on the enclosed map), which
is generally in the middle of the proposed project area. He intends to rehabilitate the
property for his business. The property is now zoned for the intended uses, and the
rehabilitation will meet all current zoning, planning and building requirements. The
rehabilitation plans also comport with the proposed plans for redevelopment.

It is clear that Ms. Fargo should not participate in matters regarding property within 300
feet of her husband's property, which have a significant fiscal impact specifically on her
interest or which relate to improvement of public services for her property. However, I
request your advisory opinion as to the following:

1. Do the owners of property within the project area constitute a "significant
segment of the public"?

2. If the owners constitute a significant segment of the public, is Ms. Fargo's interest
so distinguishable from their interests with regard to adoption of the redevelopment
plan and related matters as to require Ms. Fargo to refrain from participation?

3. If Ms. Fargo's interests are not distinguishable from the interests of the public,
are there any other issues under the facts as presented which would preclude Ms.
Fargo's participation in the redevelopment plan adoption process?

4. Without reviewing specific projects, are there issues under the facts as presented
which would preclude Ms. Fargo from participating in the implementation of the
redevelopment plan if it is adopted?

5. If it is determined that Ms. Fargo should generally refrain from participation in
the plan adoption and implementation process, would she be permitted to request
changes in the redistricting plan which would remove the project area from her
jurisdiction?

SACRAMENTO HOUSING AND REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY
John W. Wallace, FPPC
October 3, 1991
Page 3

I appreciate your attention to this matter. I would be pleased to provide you with any
additional information at my disposal. You may reach me at 916-440-1330.

Sincerely,

Dana W. Phillips

cc:    Heather Fargo, City Council Member
       Ted Kobey, Assistant City Attorney                                    ENC,

SACRAMENTO
HOUSING AND REDEVELOPMENT
AGENCY

October 4, 1991

John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: Request for Informal Assistance, Clarifying Issues Raised in your August 5, 1991
Letter to Council Member Fargo (Your File No. I-91-089)

Dear Mr. Wallace,

Please find the enclosures which were unintentionally omitted from the letter sent to you
yesterday. For reference see letter enclosed.

Sincerely,

DANA W. PHILLIPS
General Counsel

DWP:dkt

MAILING ADDRESS: P.O. Box 1834, Sacramento, CA 95812-1834
OFFICE LOCATION: 630 I Street, Sacramento, CA 95814 (916) 444-9210

sections.question:
1. Do the owners of property within the project area constitute a "significant segment of the Public"?

2. If the owners constitute a significant segment of the public, is Ms. Fargo's interest so distinguishable from their interests with regard to adoption of the redevelopment plan and related matters as to require Ms. Fargo to refrain from participation?

3. If Ms. Fargo's interests are not distinguishable from the interests of the public, are there any other issues under the facts as presented which would preclude Ms. Fargo's participation in the redevelopment plan adoption process?

4. Without reviewing specific projects, are there issues under the facts as presented which would preclude Ms. Fargo from participating in the implementation of the redevelopment plan if it is adopted?

5. If it is determined that Ms. Fargo should generally refrain from participation in the plan adoption and implementation process, would she be permitted to request changes in the redistricting plan which would remove the project area from her jurisdiction?
sections.question_synthetic: Council Member Heather Fargo's husband is acquiring property within a proposed North Sacramento redevelopment project area. The requester seeks clarification on whether Ms. Fargo has a conflict of interest that would prevent her from voting on the redevelopment plan adoption and implementation, given that her interest in the matter may or may not be distinguishable from other property owners in the area.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from the Sacramento Housing and Redevelopment Agency to the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The document includes a follow-up letter from October 4, 1991 with enclosures and a map. No conclusion or formal FPPC response is included in the provided text. The document references a prior FPPC letter from August 5, 1991 but that letter is not included in the provided text.

2. If the owners constitute a significant segment of the public, is Ms. Fargo's interest so distinguishable from their interests with regard to adoption of the redevelopment plan and related matters as to require Ms. Fargo to refrain from participation?

3. If Ms. Fargo's interests are not distinguishable from the interests of the public, are there any other issues under the facts as presented which would preclude Ms. Fargo's participation in the redevelopment plan adoption process?

4. Without reviewing specific projects, are there issues under the facts as presented which would preclude Ms. Fargo from participating in the implementation of the redevelopment plan if it is adopted?

5. If it is determined that Ms. Fargo should generally refrain from participation in the plan adoption and implementation process, would she be permitted to request changes in the redistricting plan which would remove the project area from her jurisdiction?

embedding.summary: Sacramento Housing and Redevelopment Agency requests informal FPPC advice regarding Council Member Heather Fargo's conflict of interest in voting on a redevelopment plan where her husband owns property within the proposed project area. The request asks whether Fargo's interest differs sufficiently from other property owners to require her recusal from plan adoption and implementation decisions.
